data.narrative:
SpectralX is a Series A defense and security company founded by IDF special-forces veterans. The peer universe consists of 10 comparable companies (2 public, 8 private) in the same sector and stage, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Anduril Industries, alongside public comparables DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

The valuation analysis relies on peer-median methodology across four same-sector, same-stage private companies. The peer set yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $150M (Ares Industries) to $29.0B (Scale AI). This wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity typical of Series A defense-tech funding; the $29.0B outlier (Scale AI) suggests either exceptional market positioning or data anomalies. The median of $400M anchors the central estimate and is less distorted by the extreme high end than a mean would be.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M to $460M, centered on $400M, derived from peer-median analysis. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable methodology with limited input variability. Key uncertainties include the validity of Scale AI as a peer given its $29B valuation, which may reflect a different market segment or narrative; SpectralX's specific technology differentiation and addressable market relative to the peer set; and revenue or customer traction not captured in valuation multiples alone. Additional data on revenue, unit economics, or contract pipeline would materially sharpen this estimate.
